design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot Solvalou starship vertically through 16 connected areas, switching between zapper (air targets) and blaster (ground targets) to destroy Xevious forces while navigating geographic obstacles
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dual-weapon management: choosing when to engage air vs ground threats while maintaining flight control and resource awareness
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite pilot commanding advanced attack craft to defend Earth against alien invasion, discovering hidden structures and mastering adaptive difficulty
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pioneering vertically-scrolling shooter that established template for the genre; designer Masanobu Endō deliberately crafted integrated world-building and narrative as core design goal (not tacked-on), with difficulty that adapts to player skill by replacing enemy types when mastered. Emphasis on detailed sprite work and geographic variety as narrative elements.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with extra-life collectibles (yellow Special Flags from Rally-X); configurable via debug menu (3/1/2/5 lives)
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint at ~70% through each area; dying restarts player at beginning of next area
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Adaptive difficulty responds to player mastery: game automatically escalates enemy types when player succeeds against current type; player can intentionally destroy Zolback radar towers to revert to easier enemies, providing explicit difficulty control mechanism

progression.unlock_mechanism: 16 connected areas unlock sequentially; certain areas feature optional boss encounters (Andor Genesis mothership) and hidden Sol Citadels discoverable via bombing specific ground locations
replayability_hooks: Hidden Sol Citadels require exploration and bombing knowledge
Semi-random Special Flag placement for extra lives
Adaptive difficulty encourages mastery and re-engagement with escalated challenges
Loop structure: all 16 areas cycle after completion, enabling extended play

world_building_techniques: Geographic variety: forests, roads, rivers, mechanical structures establish alien world
Nazca lines Easter eggs on ground (condor designs) provide world lore hints
Enemy design homages to Star Wars, UFO, Alien, Battlestar Galactica create sci-fi cohesion
Named locations and structures (Solvalou ship, Andor Genesis mothership, Zolback radars, Sol Citadels) establish consistent terminology
Character sprites designed as integral visual narrative elements with high detail work

boss_design.philosophy: Milestone encounters tied to geographic progression; Andor Genesis mothership serves as recurring strategic challenge featuring projectile management and multiple destruction paths
notable_bosses: Andor Genesis mothership: launches endless projectiles and explosive black spheres (Zakatos); player can destroy either all four blaster receptacles or central core to defeat

faction_indicators: Xevious forces: alien invaders plotting takeover of Earth
danger_signals: Flashing-red Zolback radars: indicate enemy escalation progression
Zakatos projectiles: endless threat pattern from mothership
Progressive area difficulty: escalating enemy types signal player advancement into hostile territory
environmental_storytelling: Geographic progression through 16 distinct Earth locations shows expanding alien occupation
Ground installations and vehicles represent military infrastructure under attack
Mechanical structures vs natural terrain suggest alien colonization overlay on human world
Sol Citadels hidden in landscape indicate pre-existing defense infrastructure or alien bases
